narrative:
Alessandro Circati is a highly-rated Centre-Back representing Australia. His overall rating of 7.46/10 positions him in the top 1% of centre-backs at this tournament. This impressive rating is 75% derived from his consistent club form over the last two seasons. He has played 390 minutes at this World Cup.

Circati demonstrates strong defensive capabilities, evident from his 2.308 tackles per 90 minutes and 4.385 clearances per 90 minutes at this World Cup. His offensive output is notably lower, with just 0.129 expected goals and 0.018 expected assists per 90 minutes in the tournament. These figures highlight a player primarily focused on defensive duties.

Circati clearly plays a crucial role as a defensive anchor for his national team. Scouts and fans should continue to watch for his robust defensive contributions in remaining matches. His consistent high performance, reflected in his 7.08–7.83 rating range, marks him as a key player.
